name: InternVL2.5-78B
license: Qwen License (InternViT component MIT; Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct component under Qwen License)
archDisplay: InternViT-6B vision encoder + Qwen2.5-72B language model (dense composite MLLM)
note: First open-source MLLM to exceed 70% on MMMU, rivaling GPT-4o while trained on far fewer tokens than competitors.
tpsNote: 78B model exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4 (~53GB needed); no single-4090 measurement exists.

results.notes: AI2D test, without-mask setting (89.1 with-mask also reported)
TextVQA val; exceeds the textvqa slug's stated 40-75 range but is the verbatim published number
MathVision full test set (mini split scored 34.9)
MMBench v1.0 CN test; exceeds the mmbench-cn slug's stated 40-82 range but is the verbatim published number
RefCOCO val split (testA 95.6, testB 92.5 also reported); slightly exceeds the slug's stated 50-92 range
Video-MME without subtitles (74.0 with subtitles also reported)

RefCOCO+ val split, from Table 6 of the InternVL3 technical report (arXiv:2504.10479), which lists InternVL2.5-78B as a comparison baseline (same organization, OpenGVLab, prior model generation). Same table also lists testA 94.7 and testB 86.9 for this model.

CharXiv reasoning-question (RQ) subset, matching the corpus convention (see e.g. amazon-nova-premier.json, cohere/command-a-plus.json) of using the reasoning subset for the charxiv slug. Descriptive-question (DQ) subset was 82.3, not used. Not previously tracked for this model.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real: release date Dec 5, 2024 confirmed via internvl.github.io blog post. Fetched HF model card directly — confirms 78B total, architecture is InternViT-6B + Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct + MLP projector, and license is MIT for the project/InternViT component with Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct governed by the separate Qwen License (matches record). Fetched the InternVL 2.5 blog page directly and confirmed all four benchmark numbers verbatim: MMMU 70.1, MathVista 72.3, DocVQA 95.1, OCRBench 854. CAVEAT: ctxK=32 (32K) is inferred from the Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct backbone's native context length, not an explicit, directly-stated context spec for the multimodal InternVL2.5-78B checkpoint itself — the cited GitHub issue is an unanswered question, not a confirmation, and other InternVL documentation suggests InternVL2.5 models were officially evaluated at 16K rather than 32K. Kept the value since it is a defensible LLM-backbone-derived estimate, but confidence is moderate rather than high.
